_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning how printed and digital texts are organised using features like page numbers, tables of contents, headings and titles.

_source.w: We are learning how printed and digital texts are organised using features like page numbers, tables of contents, headings and titles.
y: Text features help us find our way around a book or website quickly.
When we know how texts are set up, we can find information without reading every word.
Understanding these features makes us smarter, more efficient readers.
eg: We use a table of contents to find a chapter about a topic we want to read.
We look at page numbers to find a specific page in a book.
We use headings to scan a text and decide which part to read first.
We explore a simple digital text and find the links that take us to different sections.
We compare a printed book with a digital text and find similar features in both.
We create a table of contents for our own class book.
